content.text:
Installing the VSIX from that path. Checking that the file exists and which CLI can install it.

[REDACTED]

input.command: Test-Path "d:\我的资料库\Documents\Downloads\cursor-free-2.4.11.vsix"; Get-Item "d:\我的资料库\Documents\Downloads\cursor-free-2.4.11.vsix" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object FullName, Length
where.exe cursor 2>$null; where.exe code 2>$null; Get-Command cursor -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object Source; Get-Command code -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object Source
if (Test-Path "d:\我的资料库\Documents\Downloads\cursor-free-2.4.11.vsix") { Write-Host "EXISTS"; (Get-Item "d:\我的资料库\Documents\Downloads\cursor-free-2.4.11.vsix").Length } else { Write-Host "NOT FOUND" }
input.description: Check VSIX exists with full permissions
Test shell output with all permissions

input.prompt:
Install a VSIX extension on Windows. The file path is: d:\我的资料库\Documents\Downloads\cursor-free-2.4.11.vsix

Steps:
1. Verify the file exists
2. Find Cursor CLI - common paths: %LOCALAPPDATA%\Programs\cursor\resources\app\bin\cursor.cmd, %LOCALAPPDATA%\Programs\Cursor\resources\app\bin\cursor.cmd
3. Run: cursor --install-extension "d:\我的资料库\Documents\Downloads\cursor-free-2.4.11.vsix"
   Or use full path to cursor.cmd if not on PATH
4. If cursor fails, try code CLI similarly
5. Return the full command output and whether installation succeeded
